title: Delicate Fluffy Egyptian Lentil Pilaf
description: A savory and fluffy Egyptian dish featuring dill and made with ayurvedic tamasic friendly ingredients, perfect for a midnight craving.
instructions: Rinse the lentils and soak them in water for 30 minutes.
Drain and rinse the lentils again, then set them aside.
Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the soaked lentils, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the water to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes.
Meanwhile, prepare the dill by pounding it in a mortar and pestle to release its oils and flavor.
Fluff the cooked lentils with a fork and stir in the chopped dill and cooked basmati rice.
Serve the pilaf hot, garnished with additional chopped fresh dill if desired.
